title: AN ACT CREATING A BARANGAY TO BE KNOWN AS BARANGAY DON ALBINO T. TARUC IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF SOCORRO, PROVINCE OF SURIGAO DEL NORTE

chunks.text:
115 OG No. 8, 1826 (February 25, 2019); Manila Bulletin, February 11, 2019

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 11191, January 10, 2019 ]

AN ACT CREATING A BARANGAY TO BE KNOWN AS BARANGAY DON ALBINO T. TARUC IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF SOCORRO, PROVINCE OF SURIGAO DEL NORTE

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled;

SECTION 1.

Creation of Barangay Don Albino T. Taruc. -

Puroks I, II, III and IV are hereby separated from Barangay Navarro and constituted into a new barangay to be known as Barangay Don Albino T. Taruc in the Municipality of Socorro, Province of Surigao del Norte.

SEC. 2.

Territorial Boundaries. -

The terrotirial boundaries of Barangay Don Albino T. Taruc shall be identified as follows:

Bounded on the Southwest (SW), Northwest (NW) and Northeast (NE) along lines 31-1 to 16 by Barangay Navarro Case 1, Socorro, Surigao del Norte.

On the East (E) along lines 16 to 31 by the Pacific Ocean.

The technical description of Barangay Don Albino T. Taruc is a follows:

SEC. 3.

Conduct and Supervision of Plebiscite. -

The plebiscite conducted and supervised by the Commission on Elections (COMELEC) on August 27, 1995 pursuant to Provincial Ordinance No. 001, Series of 1995 of Sangguniang Panlalawigan of the Province of Surigao del Norte, which ratified the creation of Barangay Don Albino T. Taruc, shall serve as substantial compliance with the plebiscite requirement under Section 10 of Republic Act No. 7160, as amended, otherwise known as the "Local Government Code of 1991".

SEC. 4.

Incumbent Barangay Official. -

The incumbent barangay officials of Barangay Don Albino T. Taruc shall continue to hold office until such time that their successors have been duly elected and qualified.

SEC. 5.

Public Infrastructure and Facilities. -

All public infrastructure and facilities for public use existing therein at the time of the approval of this Act are hereby transferred without cost or compensation to and shall be administered by the new barangay.

SEC. 6.

Internal Revenue Allotment Shares. -

Barangay Don Albino T. Taruc shall be entitled to Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) shares pursuant to Section 285 of Republic Act No. 7160, as amended, otherwise known as the "Local Government Code of 1991".

SEC. 7.

Effectivity. -

This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette

or in a newspaper of general circulation.

Approved,

(SGD.)

VICENTE C. SOTTO III

President of the Senate

(SGD.)

GLORIA MACAPAGAL-ARROYO

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was passed by the House of Representatives on March 8, 2017, amended by the Senate on August 14, 2017, and which amendments were concurred in by the House of Representatives on November 12, 2018.

(SGD.)

MYRA MARIE D. VILLARICA

Secretary of the Senate

(SGD.)

DANTE ROBERTO P. MALING

Acting Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved: JAN 10 2019

(SGD.)

RODRIGO ROA DUTERTE

President of the Philippines
